{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Grob's Attack| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pd| | | |= | | | | | | |pl| |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1.g4 e5 |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A00]] }} =Grob's Attack= ===1. g4 e5=== This move is interesting not because it is a commonly-played move, nor because it gives an advantage to either player (though Black should still be far better), but because White has not one, but ''two'' moves that lead to an early checkmate for Black. [[/2. f4|2. f4??]] transposes to the From Gambit of the Bird’s Opening, but this matters little because black can easily mate. [[/2. f3|2. f3??]] leads to the same early demise for white, this time transposing to the Barnes Opening. Both these blunders are called Fool's Mate, and tie for the fastest possible checkmate for black. Although Grob's Attack is itself rarely used among serious players, [[/2. d3|2. d3]] is a more serious alternative. White will still be objectively worse, but can put up a good fight in spite of their weak kingside, and lack of resistance in the centre. There is also [[Chess Opening Theory/1. g4/1...e5/2. Bg2|2. Bg2]] which attempts to get into a psuedo-King's Indian with the g pawn able to kick out a potential f6 knight. == Win percentages == === Masters === White wins: 36% | Draw: 15% | Black wins: 48% === Lichess === White wins: 48% | Draw: 3% | Black wins: 49% ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1.g4 e5'''
